data.title: Mohawk Industries outlines $32M in new annual savings and projects Q4 EPS of $1.90-$2 amid market headwinds
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Mohawk Industries (MHK) Q3 2025

Management View

  • CEO Jeff Lorberbaum reported "our third quarter net sales of $2.8 billion were in line with our expectations, slightly ahead of prior year as reported and flat on a constant basis." He emphasized that "though economic conditions across our regions weakened more than anticipated compared to the prior quarter, we believe we outperformed in most of our markets." The CEO highlighted that sales and product mix benefited from premium residential and commercial offerings, with adjusted earnings per share of $2.67 reflecting ongoing productivity and restructuring initiatives, favorable currency exchange, and lower interest expense, partially offset by higher input costs and temporary plant shutdowns.
  • Lorberbaum announced, "we've identified additional restructuring opportunities to rationalize less efficient assets and streamline logistics operations and administrative functions. These new actions will result in annualized savings of approximately $32 million at a net cash cost of approximately $20 million after asset sales." He added, "combined with our previously announced restructuring actions, we anticipate delivering $110 million in savings this year."
  • The CEO noted, "we repurchased 315,000 shares in the quarter for approximately $40 million as part of our current stock buyback authorization. Year-to-date, we've purchased $108 million of our outstanding shares."
  • CFO James Brunk stated, "sales for the quarter were just shy of $2.8 billion. That's a 1.4% increase as reported and flat on a constant basis as our hard surface and commercial business continued to outperform the overall residential channels."
  • Brunk detailed, "gross profit for the quarter was 23.7% as reported and 25.3%, excluding charges, as strengthening productivity of $57 million and favorable impact of FX of $15 million were offset by higher input costs of $39 million, continued pressure on price/mix of $20 million and lower volume and temporary shutdown costs of $23 million."
  • President and COO Paul De Cock noted, "in the Global Ceramics segment, our performance benefited from our premium collections, commercial sales and expanded distribution. All of our markets faced pricing pressure due to excess industry capacity, though we were able to offset due to the strength of our product and channel mix."

Outlook

  • Lorberbaum said, "given these factors, we expect our fourth quarter EPS to be between $1.90 and $2 with 1 additional shipping day and excluding any restructuring or other onetime charges."
  • Management indicated that "market volume should remain soft through the end of the year" and ongoing restructuring is expected to deliver further cost savings.
  • Brunk forecasted "in Q4 and for the full year a tax rate of approximately 18%."

Financial Results

  • Adjusted earnings per share for Q3 was $2.67. Earnings per share as reported was $1.75.
  • Free cash flow for the quarter was $310 million.
  • Global Ceramic sales were just over $1.1 billion; Flooring North America sales were $937 million; Flooring Rest of the World sales were $716 million.
  • Operating income on an adjusted basis for Global Ceramic was $90 million, for Flooring North America $68 million, and for Flooring Rest of the World $59 million.
  • CapEx for the quarter was $76 million. Cash and cash equivalents were $516 million.
  • Brunk commented, "overall, the balance sheet is in a very strong position with gross debt of $1.9 billion and leverage at 1.1x."

Q&A

  • John Lovallo, UBS: Asked about changes since July affecting Q4 EPS outlook and revenue/margin expectations. Brunk responded, "conditions did weaken since we last talked during the quarter with interest rates remaining elevated. The other aspect is consumer confidence decline, which affected our remodeling. Builders have actually slowed a little bit from a construction standpoint and international markets have softened. Inflation has eased, but our costs are still higher than the prior year."
  • Matthew Bouley, Barclays: Questioned price increases related to tariffs. De Cock answered, "we have also announced in the third quarter additional price increases, both to recover the tariffs and to recover inflation in carpet. For the tariffs, we announced an additional price increase between 5% and 10%."
  • Collin Verron, Deutsche Bank: Asked about raw material cost declines and margin impact. Brunk replied, "in the fourth quarter, we will see raw material prices easing from their peak earlier in the year. Energy and wages will continue to be higher than last year."
  • Susan Maklari, Goldman Sachs: Inquired about new product launches and their momentum. Lorberbaum explained, "every one of the businesses has product innovation coming through it... In the LVT collections, they are updating the decorating trend as well as we're introducing PVC alternatives that we just lump into a group we call hybrid."
  • Adam Baumgarten, Vertical Research: Asked if the industry is now more coordinated from a tariff-driven price increase perspective. Lorberbaum: "I'm not sure it's coordinated. The whole industry has the same impacts from the tariffs going up... we're assuming it will take until the first of the year for it to level out."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed slightly negative to neutral sentiment, focusing on tariff impacts, price increases, and cost structure, with some concern over demand and margin trajectory. There was persistent questioning about the timeline for margin recovery and competitive dynamics.
  • Management maintained a measured but confident tone in prepared remarks, emphasizing cost reductions, productivity gains, and readiness for market recovery. In Q&A, responses were occasionally defensive or cautious, especially regarding demand visibility and tariff pass-throughs, as seen with phrases like "we believe..." and "we hope...".
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst and management tone both shifted to more cautious and defensive, with greater emphasis on macroeconomic headwinds and the timing of recovery.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q3 guidance for Q4 EPS ($1.90-$2) is lower than the Q2 guidance for Q3 EPS ($2.56-$2.66), reflecting a more cautious outlook due to weaker demand and ongoing cost pressures.
  • Management increased total annualized restructuring savings targets to $110 million, up from $100 million last quarter, with an additional $32 million in new annualized savings initiatives announced.
  • Free cash flow improved to $310 million from $125 million in Q2, and the share repurchase program continued.
  • Strategic focus remains on restructuring, cost reduction, and product innovation, but management now projects persistently soft volume through year-end, versus prior quarter's hope for sequential improvement.
  • Analysts’ questions shifted more heavily toward tariffs, price/mix recovery, and cost inflation, with less emphasis on new product momentum.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited ongoing consumer uncertainty and postponement of large renovation projects as key headwinds.
  • Tariff impacts remain a significant risk, with management implementing price increases and supply chain optimization but noting it will take time for the market to reach equilibrium.
  • Energy, wage, and material costs remain elevated compared to last year.
  • Analysts raised concerns about timing and adequacy of price increases, competitive dynamics, and potential for further demand deterioration.

Final Takeaway

Management reported that Mohawk Industries is navigating a challenging market environment with targeted restructuring actions, new product introductions, and supply chain optimizations to address persistent input cost pressures and tariff impacts. While the company anticipates soft demand to continue through the end of the year, ongoing operational enhancements, annualized savings of $110 million, and an expected fourth quarter EPS range of $1.90 to $2 signal a commitment to maintaining financial strength and positioning the business for recovery when market conditions improve.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Plymouth Industrial agrees to be acquired by Makarora for $2.1B
data.description:
Entities tied to Makarora Management, along with Ares Alternative Credit funds, have agreed to acquire all outstanding shares of Plymouth Industrial REIT (NYSE:PLYM) in all all-cash deal valued at about $2.1B, Plymouth said on Friday.

All outstanding shares of Plymouth (NYSE:PLYM) and all outstanding limited partnership interests of Plymouth Industrial OP, LP will be purchased for $22.00 per share. 

Plymouth (PLYM) said it plans to conduct a 30-day go-shop period that will expire Nov. 23, 2025, which permits the company and its financial advisors to actively initiate, solicit and consider alternative acquisition proposals from third parties.

The purchase price represented a ~50% premium to Plymouth’s unaffected closing common stock price on Aug. 18, 2025, the last trading day prior to the filing by affiliates of Sixth Street Partners disclosing a non-binding proposal to acquire all the outstanding shares of Plymouth’s common stock.

PLYM closed Friday's trading session at $22.07 per share, before slipping 1.3% in after-hours trading.

The deal is expected to close early next year, subject to approval by PLYM shareholders.

data.title: CCL Industries announces $19M bolt-on acquisition for avery
data.description:
• CCL Industries (OTCPK:CCLLF) announced on Friday that it has acquired IDESCO Holding Corporation and IDSecurityonline.com, privately owned providers of secure badging and identification solutions based in Manhattan, New York. 
  • Sales for the year 2024 were approximately $21.5M with an estimated 14.2% adjusted EBIT margin. 
  • The debt-free, all-cash deal totals $19M, subject to customary closing conditions.

data.title: Cenntro receives 180 days to regain Nasdaq compliance
data.description:
• Cenntro (NASDAQ:CENN) announced on Friday that it has been granted an additional 180 calendar day extension from Nasdaq to regain compliance with the Nasdaq’s minimum $1.00 closing bid price requirement.
  • The company now has until April 20, 2026, to meet the minimum bid price requirement.
  • CENN +2.93% after hours to $0.2207.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: SEI increases stock share repurchase program by $650M
data.description:
• SEI Investments Company (NASDAQ:SEIC) said on Friday that its board has approved an increase in its stock repurchase program by an additional $650M. 
  • This has increased the available authorization under the program to approximately $773.2M, which includes $123.2M as of October 20, 2025, remaining under the company's existing share repurchase authorization.

data.title: Illinois Tool Works narrows 2025 EPS guidance to $10.40–$10.50 as margin improvement continues
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Illinois Tool Works Inc. (ITW) Q3 2025

Management View

  • President and CEO Christopher O'Herlihy indicated that the company delivered solid operational and financial execution in Q3 2025, outpacing underlying end market demand and achieving organic growth of 1%, a 1-percentage-point improvement from Q2. He stated, "For the third quarter, revenue increased 3%, excluding a 1% reduction related to our ongoing strategic product line simplification efforts. Organic growth was 1%, a solid performance relative to end markets that we estimate declined low single digits." O'Herlihy highlighted a GAAP EPS of $2.81, a 6% increase in operating income to a record $1.1 billion, and a 90 basis point improvement in operating margin to 27.4%.
  • O'Herlihy pointed to the company’s 62nd consecutive dividend increase and over $1.1 billion in share repurchases year-to-date. He emphasized progress on strategic growth priorities and reaffirmed the focus on above-market organic growth through customer-backed innovation: "The strategy is working, and we remain firmly on track to deliver on our 2030 performance goals, which include customer-backed innovation yield of 3% plus."
  • CFO Michael Larsen reported, "Total revenue increased by more than 2%, driven in part by 1% organic growth, an improvement of 1 percentage point from Q2." He noted Asia Pacific as a standout with a 7% increase, including 10% growth in China. Larsen added, "Free cash flow grew 15% to more than $900 million with a conversion rate of 110%. GAAP EPS was $2.81 with an effective tax rate in the quarter of 21.8%."

Outlook

  • Management maintained full-year organic growth guidance of 0% to 2% and total revenue growth of 1% to 3%. Larsen stated, "We are narrowing the range of our guidance to a new range of $10.40 to $10.50" for GAAP EPS, including a benefit from a lower projected tax rate of approximately 23% for the full year. He highlighted that the top line is trending towards the lower end of the revenue guidance range.
  • Operating margin guidance remains at 26% to 27%, with enterprise initiatives expected to contribute 125 basis points for the full year. Management reiterated confidence in meeting the $10.45 midpoint EPS target.

Financial Results

  • The company reported Q3 revenue up 3% (excluding a 1% product line simplification reduction), organic growth of 1%, and a favorable 2% impact from foreign currency. Operating margin reached 27.4%. GAAP EPS was $2.81, and free cash flow surpassed $900 million, reflecting a 15% increase and a 110% conversion rate.
  • Segment highlights included Automotive OEM leading with 7% revenue growth and 5% organic growth, with China up 10%. Automotive OEM operating margin improved 240 basis points to 21.8%. The Food Equipment segment saw a 3% revenue increase, and Welding delivered 3% organic growth, with equipment sales up 6%. Construction Products’ revenues declined 1%, but operating margin increased 140 basis points to 31.6%.

Q&A

  • Jeffrey Sprague, Vertical Research Partners: Asked about construction margins and sustainability. O'Herlihy responded, "The margins in construction are squarely related to the quality of the construction portfolio... We are executing very well from a business model perspective against those particular parts of the market."
  • Sprague followed up on visibility for Test & Measurement improvement in Q4. O'Herlihy indicated expectations for cyclical improvement and noted a slowdown in Q3 attributed to tariff uncertainty.
  • Andrew Kaplowitz, Citi: Inquired about organic revenue growth guidance and Q4 expectations. Larsen explained, "We are trending towards the lower end of the organic growth guidance for the full year. We typically see a sequential improvement from Q3 to Q4... primarily driven by the Test & Measurement business."
  • Jamie Cook, Truist: Sought clarification on FX and tax rate effects in guidance. Larsen noted, "In Q3, FX was favorable $0.04. But then other things like restructuring were unfavorable by a couple of pennies."
  • Tami Zakaria, JPMorgan: Asked about onshoring in auto and Product Line Simplification (PLS). O'Herlihy stated, "Business coming back to the U.S. would just mean more production for our U.S. factories, but they're already here."
  • Other analysts focused on margin sustainability, capital deployment, price/cost dynamics, China growth drivers, and the potential for segment-level improvements.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts’ tone was neutral to slightly cautious, with questions centering on the sustainability of margin gains, visibility on end-market improvements, and factors affecting guidance. Skepticism appeared around the ability to maintain or grow margins in uncertain demand environments.
  • Management’s prepared remarks conveyed confidence and a positive outlook. During Q&A, the tone became marginally more cautious, particularly regarding demand softness and the rationale for maintaining guidance ranges. Larsen used phrases such as "a more measured, a more cautious approach to our guidance here as we go into Q4."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analysts’ skepticism increased regarding demand trends and margin sustainability, while management’s tone shifted from outright confidence to cautious optimism, particularly when discussing end-market uncertainties and the rationale for not raising guidance further.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The company’s guidance language narrowed from Q2, with EPS guidance now at $10.40–$10.50 versus a previously broader range. Organic growth guidance remains unchanged at 0% to 2%, but management emphasized the lower end of the range this quarter.
  • Strategic focus remains on margin expansion, customer-backed innovation, and product line simplification. Management reiterated confidence in achieving long-term goals but spoke more cautiously about near-term demand trends.
  • Analysts’ questions in Q3 were more focused on the sustainability of current performance amid persistent end-market softness, a shift from Q2’s more optimistic tone on sequential improvements.
  • Key metric improvements this quarter included operating margin (up 90 basis points sequentially) and continued strong free cash flow, while challenges persisted in Construction Products and Polymers & Fluids.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted a "stable yet still challenging demand environment" and acknowledged choppiness in monthly demand, particularly in automotive and Test & Measurement segments.
  • Tariff-related cost increases remain a risk, but pricing and supply chain actions are expected to offset these headwinds. Management noted, "We've been able to, through those actions, offset the impact from tariffs this year."
  • Analysts raised concerns about market demand, potential impacts of pricing actions, and the sustainability of margin expansion in the face of continued end-market weakness.

Final Takeaway

Illinois Tool Works management emphasized strong margin improvement, robust free cash flow, and above-market growth in key segments despite mixed demand conditions. The company reaffirmed its commitment to strategic growth priorities, maintained its organic growth and margin targets, and narrowed EPS guidance to $10.40–$10.50 for 2025. While management remains confident in delivering on long-term objectives, caution prevails regarding near-term market uncertainties and the choppiness of demand in certain segments.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Newmont weighing deal for Barrick’s Nevada gold assets - Bloomberg
data.description:
Newmont (NYSE:NEM) is considering a potential deal to gain control of rival Barrick Mining's (NYSE:B) prized Nevada gold assets, although it is unclear how receptive Barrick would be to any overtures, Bloomberg reported Friday.

Newmont (NEM) already owns a minority stake in a Nevada gold mining joint venture with operator Barrick (B), but Newmont reportedly is in the early stages of considering various transaction structures that would allow it to gain full ownership of the assets.

Newmont (NEM) could consider options including a bid for Barrick's (B) stake in the JV as well as a full takeover of the company followed by divestments of assets it views as non-core, according to the report.

The company is said to be less interested in Barrick's (B) African operations and the Reko Diq copper-gold project in Pakistan.

Newmont (NEM) shares closed -6.2% on Friday, as better than expected Q3 earnings were overshadowed by concerns over weaker production and its forecast for a modest drop in gold production next year due to planned mine sequencing at key operations.

Separately, Newmont (NEM) said its Ahafo North project in Ghana has achieved commercial production, marking the successful completion of "one of West Africa's most significant recent mining developments."
